---
title: "Transgenic Waste"
type: "protocol"
---
# Transgenic Waste Disposal

*Created by vianneyahn on 2026-04-21*

As our lab works with transgenic materials (BSL1), all transgenic waste must be autoclaved prior to disposal.

If you have not already done so, please complete the UC Davis Biosafety Training Course [here](https://safetyservices.ucdavis.edu/training/safe-use-of-biological-safety-cabinets).

In general, we collect transgenic waste in double-bagged autoclave bags (clear bags, located in the drawer next to the sink and in the Asmundson walk-in growth chamber). Waste should be autoclaved at 120 °C for a minimum of 30 minutes in a secondary container. After autoclaving, it can be disposed of in the regular trash bins located outside the Robbins building.

If a large volume of waste accumulates, you can instead bring it to the [Orchard Park Greenhouses](https://maps.app.goo.gl/Z7pAvknNv2F8k7M77). An orange transgenic waste bin is located at the end of headhouse #3, and waste is steamed weekly on Monday afternoons.

<br>
![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

They seem to lock the gates after 5pm on weekdays and on weekends, but you can also access the greenhouses using the gate lock code 5184.

Reach out to the greenhouse manager Rosie Gluck at [rlgluck@ucdavis.edu](mailto:rlgluck@ucdavis.edu) for more information.
